summary: Completed testing of Country Auto-Detection feature. All tests passed - IP detection via ipapi.co works correctly, navbar shows country flag indicator (🇺🇸 US) on both desktop and mobile, localStorage caching works with 24h TTL, and registration forms pre-select the detected country when cache is available.
passed_tests: Homepage: Country indicator visible in desktop navbar (data-testid='country-indicator') - PASSED
Homepage: Country indicator shows '🇺🇸US' (detected from IP via ipapi.co) - PASSED
Homepage: Country indicator visible on transparent navbar (homepage) - PASSED
Homepage: Country indicator visible on solid navbar (after scroll) - PASSED
Mobile: Country indicator visible in mobile menu with flag and code - PASSED
Search page: Country indicator visible on solid navbar - PASSED
Categories page: Country indicator visible on solid navbar - PASSED
LocalStorage: Country cached with key 'bookvia_detected_country' - PASSED
LocalStorage: Cache contains {code: 'US', ts: timestamp} - PASSED
User Registration: Country selector pre-selects US when cache available - PASSED
User Registration: Phone prefix updates to +1 (US) - PASSED
Business Registration: Country selector pre-selects US when cache available - PASSED
Business Registration: Phone prefix updates to +1 (US) - PASSED
No JavaScript console errors detected - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: detectCountry.js implementation is well-structured with IP detection, timezone fallback, and localStorage caching
Country indicator in Navbar uses proper data-testid attribute for testing
getDetectedCountry() is synchronous - returns cached value or timezone fallback, which is correct design
Registration forms correctly use getDetectedCountry() for initial state
24h cache TTL is appropriate for country detection

mocked_api_note.ipapi.co: External API - working correctly in preview environment

test_credentials: No credentials needed - tested public pages only
context_for_next_testing_agent: Country auto-detection feature is fully implemented and tested. Key points: (1) detectCountry() is async and calls ipapi.co/json with 3s timeout, (2) getDetectedCountry() is sync and returns cached value or timezone fallback or 'MX' default, (3) Country indicator uses data-testid='country-indicator' in Navbar, (4) Cache key is 'bookvia_detected_country' in localStorage with 24h TTL, (5) Registration forms use getDetectedCountry() for initial country value - this means if user goes directly to /register without visiting homepage first, they'll see the default (MX) until cache is populated.
notes: The country indicator correctly shows US flag (🇺🇸) since the preview server is in the US. When users visit the homepage first, the async IP detection runs and caches the result. Subsequent navigation to registration pages will show the cached country. If users go directly to /register without visiting homepage, they'll see the default (Mexico) until the cache is populated on their next visit to a page with the Navbar.
